meta.title: Mercedes M137 V12 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
meta.description: The M137 is a rare 5.8L V12 flagship engine — but complex ignition and oil leaks are common. See honest replacement costs and compare specialist quotes.

@graph.name: Mercedes-Benz models fitted with the M137

mainEntity.name: How much does an M137 engine replacement cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned M137 typically costs £8,000–£14,000 supply only, varying by specific variant. Fitting adds £2,000–£4,000 depending on labour rates. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes M137 a reliable engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: The M137 is rated 2.5★ out of 5 — it was designed to be lighter and more efficient than the M120 V12 it replaced, but its complex ignition system and oil leaks are well-documented. Many specialists consider the earlier M120 to be more durable.

mainEntity.name: What is the most common M137 failure?

acceptedAnswer.text: Common issues include ignition module/coil failures (temperature-dependent misfires), oil leaks from valve cover gaskets and oil pans, timing chain tensioner wear, alternator failure, and cooling system leaks (radiator, water pump).

mainEntity.name: Which Mercedes models use the M137?

acceptedAnswer.text: The M137 was only used in the S 600 (W220) and CL 600 (C215) between 1998 and 2002. It was replaced by the M275 bi-turbo V12.

mainEntity.name: How long does the M137 last?

acceptedAnswer.text: With meticulous maintenance, the M137 can reach 100,000–150,000 miles. However, specialists note that complex V12 repairs — particularly ignition system failures — are common in this mileage range, and parts availability is becoming an increasing concern.

hero.h1: Mercedes-Benz M137 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The Mercedes M137 is a 5.8-litre V12 — a lighter, more advanced flagship engine than its predecessor, but its complex ignition system and oil leaks are well-documented. Backed by real UK enquiries from EnginesMarket.co.uk.

verdictRating.confidence: 80% confidence — based on verified specialist rebuild reports and UK workshop consensus.

rows.score: 8 (Complex V12 design; specialist-only work; many failures require full strip)
10 (Available but dealer/specialist-order only; some parts scarce)

verdictRating.oneLineVerdict: The M137 is a smooth, exclusive V12 — but its complex ignition system and oil leaks are common, and parts are increasingly scarce — buy only with full service history and a specialist inspection.
verdictRating.bestFor: Collectors and enthusiasts wanting a V12 Mercedes flagship experience.
verdictRating.avoidIf: You're on a tight maintenance budget or looking for a daily driver.

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting an M137 typically takes 14–20 hours at £75–£110/hr (independent) or £130–£180/hr (London/SE dealer).
